description:
You are a data analyst who has just received two quarterly sales reports in CSV format.  
Your goal is to create an East-region product sales summary for the first half of the year and to document, step-by-step, the actions you took.

Starting state of the system
1. Directory /home/user/data already exists and contains exactly two files:
   a) /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv  
   b) /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv  
   The precise contents of these files are shown below.

2. The directory /home/user/output is present and writable but empty.

What you must accomplish
1. Combine the two quarterly CSV files into a single data set.
2. Keep only the rows where the value of the Region column is “East”.
3. Calculate the total (sum) of the Sales column for each distinct Product that appears in the filtered data.
4. Sort the resulting product totals in descending order of TotalSales (highest first).
5. Save the final result to /home/user/output/east_sales_summary.csv with the exact format specified in “Required output file format”.
6. Create a plain-text log file /home/user/output/transformation.log that lists, in order, the high-level shell steps you executed. The log must follow the format specified in “Required log file format”.

Required output file format
• File path: /home/user/output/east_sales_summary.csv  
• The file must be a valid CSV with Unix line endings (LF).  
• It must contain exactly four lines: one header line followed by three data lines.  
• The header line must read:  
  Product,TotalSales  
• Each data line must consist of a product name (string with no embedded commas) and the associated integer total, separated by a single comma.  
• The lines must appear in this exact order: Gadget, Widget, Doohickey.  
• Example of the complete, correct file (including the trailing newline after the last line):

Product,TotalSales
Gadget,1860
Widget,1490
Doohickey,500

Required log file format
• File path: /home/user/output/transformation.log  
• Exactly five lines, each beginning with the token “STEP_” followed by a one-based step number and a colon, then a short free-text description of what happened in that step.  
• The five steps must describe, in order, the following high-level actions (wording may vary but the meaning must match):  
  1) merging the two CSV files  
  2) filtering rows for Region = East  
  3) aggregating/adding the Sales values per Product  
  4) sorting the aggregated result by TotalSales descending  
  5) writing the east_sales_summary.csv file  
• Example structure (your descriptions can differ, but the tokens “STEP_1:” … “STEP_5:” and the order are mandatory):

STEP_1: <description of merging>
STEP_2: <description of filtering>
STEP_3: <description of aggregation>
STEP_4: <description of sorting>
STEP_5: <description of writing summary file>

Constraints & expectations
• Perform the task entirely from the Linux command line; do not edit files manually with a GUI editor.  
• You are expected to complete the transformation in roughly five to ten shell commands (not enforced, but serves as a complexity guideline).  
• Do not hard-code the final numbers; they must be obtained through command-line data processing.  
• After you finish, /home/user/output must contain exactly the two new files described above and nothing else.

CSV files provided to you

/home/user/data/sales_q1.csv
Region,Product,Sales
East,Gadget,1200
West,Widget,800
East,Widget,950
North,Gadget,620
East,Doohickey,300

/home/user/data/sales_q2.csv
Region,Product,Sales
South,Gadget,730
East,Gadget,660
West,Doohickey,410
East,Widget,540
East,Doohickey,200

Verify that your commands run without error, that the two required files are present in /home/user/output, and that their contents match the formats detailed above.
truth:
Initial file system state (before the agent starts)
Directories:
- /home/user/data
- /home/user/output

Existing files:

1. /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv
Contents (LF line endings):
Region,Product,Sales
East,Gadget,1200
West,Widget,800
East,Widget,950
North,Gadget,620
East,Doohickey,300

2. /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv
Contents (LF line endings):
Region,Product,Sales
South,Gadget,730
East,Gadget,660
West,Doohickey,410
East,Widget,540
East,Doohickey,200


Expected final file system state (after successful agent completion)

Directory /home/user/output contains exactly two files:

1. /home/user/output/east_sales_summary.csv  
Contents (LF line endings, final newline present):
Product,TotalSales
Gadget,1860
Widget,1490
Doohickey,500

2. /home/user/output/transformation.log  
Contents (LF line endings) consisting of exactly five lines:
Line 1 starts with: STEP_1:
Line 2 starts with: STEP_2:
Line 3 starts with: STEP_3:
Line 4 starts with: STEP_4:
Line 5 starts with: STEP_5:
No additional lines are allowed.  
The semantic meaning of each line must correspond to the actions: merge, filter East, aggregate, sort, write summary (order strict), but wording may vary.

Automated verification steps
1. Confirm that /home/user/output/east_sales_summary.csv exists.  
2. Read the file and assert that its exact content equals the block shown above.  
3. Confirm that /home/user/output/transformation.log exists.  
4. Assert that the file contains exactly five lines, each beginning with the required STEP_n: token (n = 1-5).  
5. Verify that no files other than the two specified exist in /home/user/output.  
6. (Indirect) The agent is expected to have used no more than ~10 commands; this is not strictly enforced but may be logged for informational purposes.
